rationale: I split Earth & Environmental Sciences into 14 practitioner-facing subspecialties to balance breadth and specificity: enough cards to let researchers pick a focused area, but not so many that choices become noisy. I prioritized disciplinary units scientists actually use (Climate Science, Oceanography, Geology) and separated methods that function across fields (Remote Sensing & GIS, Earth System Modeling) so papers map cleanly to a single card. I deliberately kept policy and public-health–style work out (those route to other level-1 categories) and avoided splitting core earth-materials and geophysics too finely; the main controversial split is separating Climate Science from Paleoclimatology and from Earth System Modeling — they overlap but practitioners typically self-identify with one of those.

subspecialties.name: Climate Science
subspecialties.tagline: Atmospheric and global climate processes, change, impacts
covers: Observations and theory of global and regional climate change
Examples: radiative forcing studies, attribution, climate sensitivity, projection analyses

subspecialties.name: Atmospheric Science
subspecialties.tagline: Weather, atmospheric dynamics, clouds, aerosols, chemistry
covers: Synoptic and mesoscale meteorology, cloud physics, atmospheric chemistry
Examples: convection studies, aerosol–radiation interactions, air chemistry measurements

subspecialties.name: Oceanography
subspecialties.tagline: Physical, chemical, and biological ocean processes
covers: Circulation, biogeochemical cycles, ocean ecology and coastal dynamics
Examples: upwelling studies, circulation modeling, ocean acidification research
explicitly_excludes: Coastal aspects routed within Oceanography not separately split

subspecialties.name: Hydrology & Water Resources
subspecialties.tagline: Surface and subsurface water movement, management, quality
covers: River basin hydrology, groundwater, water quality, watershed modelling
Examples: flood modelling, groundwater contamination, urban hydrology

subspecialties.name: Geology & Earth Materials
covers: Field and lab studies of lithology, mineralogy, sedimentary processes
Examples: volcanology papers focused on petrology, basin stratigraphy, ore studies

subspecialties.name: Geophysics & Seismology
covers: Seismic studies, mantle and crustal processes, geodetics, geophysical imaging
Examples: earthquake mechanics, tomography, gravity and magnetics interpretation

subspecialties.name: Paleoclimatology & Paleoenvironment
subspecialties.tagline: Reconstructing past climates and environmental change
covers: Proxy records, ice cores, tree rings, sediment cores, paleoecology
Examples: Holocene climate reconstructions, isotope paleotemperature studies

subspecialties.name: Environmental Chemistry
subspecialties.tagline: Chemical processes in air, water, soil, and contaminants
covers: Fate and transport of pollutants, chemical transformations in environments
Examples: persistent organic pollutant studies, aquatic chemistry, atmospheric oxidation

subspecialties.name: Biogeochemistry
subspecialties.tagline: Element cycles linking biology, chemistry, and Earth processes
covers: Carbon, nitrogen, phosphorus cycles across ecosystems, soil and aquatic biogeochemistry
Examples: carbon flux studies, microbial mediation of nutrient cycles

subspecialties.name: Remote Sensing & GIS
subspecialties.tagline: Satellite, airborne sensing and spatial analysis for Earth studies
covers: Image processing, spatial analysis, LIDAR, radar, geospatial applications
Examples: land-cover change mapping, SAR interferometry, DEM generation

subspecialties.name: Earth System Modeling
subspecialties.tagline: Numerical models of coupled Earth system components
covers: Development and evaluation of climate, land–ocean–atmosphere coupled models
Examples: model intercomparison, parameterization development, model benchmarking

subspecialties.name: Environmental Engineering
subspecialties.tagline: Applied engineering for pollution control and remediation
covers: Design and assessment of treatment systems, remediation technologies, contaminant mitigation
Examples: wastewater treatment process studies, soil remediation techniques

subspecialties.name: Sustainability Science
covers: Interdisciplinary analyses of sustainability, resilience, resource use (non-policy technical focus)
Examples: lifecycle assessments, resilience metrics, socio-environmental modeling
explicitly_excludes: Public policy and policy analysis — goes to other level-1 categories

subspecialties.name: Cryosphere & Glaciology
subspecialties.tagline: Glaciers, ice sheets, permafrost, polar processes
covers: Ice dynamics, mass balance, permafrost thaw, sea-ice processes
Examples: ice-sheet modeling, glacier observations, polar climate interactions

uncovered_concerns: Coastal Science: considered as separate but merged into Oceanography to avoid duplication and keep the grid compact.
Soil Science: overlaps strongly with Biogeochemistry and Environmental Chemistry; kept under those two to reduce card count.
Urban climate/urban hydrology: treated as topics inside Climate Science or Hydrology & Water Resources rather than separate cards.
Policy and governance-focused sustainability work excluded (routes to Social Sciences / Public Health), only technical/analytical sustainability retained.
